title: system tests: preserve image annotations
body:
Fun, fun: the tests in 120-load.bats were corrupting our
test image, merely by saving and reloading it: the save
was being done using docker format, and that loses
annotations, and it turns out that annotations are
important. Their presence/absence affects the outcome
of subsequent tests.

Solution:
 - in 120-load tests, add setup/teardown that will
   preserve $IMAGE using OCI format

 - in global setup, add an emergency check to confirm
   that $IMAGE has at least some annotations. If it
   doesn't, re-fetch it.

 - in 710-kube tests, remove an invalid assertion.
   (If the desire is to make podman shut up about
   the Kubernetes-63 issue, then fine, someone can
   fix that properly later).

Fixes: #17911

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This has completely blown up. I'm lost and need help: **how can I cache an image locally, such that everything is identical to podman pull?**

`podman save` loses annotations.

`podman save --format oci` loses Digest:
```console
$ x=quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 
$ bin/podman rmi -a
$ bin/podman pull -q $x;bin/podman inspect --format '{{.Digest}}' $x
f5a99120db6452661930a1db3bf7390eec9b963f5f62c068fa32dc1d550afad3
sha256:c782d03c968791b10300fb15478f7555be560329c5182ea27cba5fc299c98911

$ bin/podman save -q --format oci-archive $x -o /tmp/foo.tar;bin/podman rmi -a;bin/podman load -q -i /tmp/foo.tar
Untagged: quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018
Deleted: f5a99120db6452661930a1db3bf7390eec9b963f5f62c068fa32dc1d550afad3
Loaded image: quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018

$ bin/podman inspect --format {{.Digest}} $x
sha256:91e392ab518b350d035b67cad85c93340c705ad4c0edca5f804e3e14fe21bcea
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ NOT THE SAME
```
My goal is to have every system test start with a 100% guaranteed identical known good image. Is this possible?
Oh, interesting idea! I dismissed `docker://...` because it would take sooooo looonng (multiarch). But I will play with it tomorrow. Thank you for the suggestion!
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
oh, believe me, I haven't forgotten! This one is just very, very hard.

PR needs rebase.
